name: Serra de Galliners Loop
name_original: Ruta del evento OPEN NATURA SANT QUIRZE VALLES
description: This moderate 21.8-mile mountain biking route in Serra de Galliners offers varied terrain and panoramic views of the Vallès region.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Sant Quirze Del Vallès are set within a diverse landscape, characterized by mountainous terrain, forests, and varied elevation. The region features natural areas like the Serra de Galliners, offering a network of trails suitable for mountain biking. Riders can explore routes that traverse both wooded sections and open areas, providing panoramic views of the Vallès region. The area's geography includes a mix of ascents and descents, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Sant Quirze Del Vallès The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra de Galliners Loop, a moderate 21.8 miles (35.0 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain and panoramic views across the Vallès region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pi d'en Xandri – Sant Medir Hermitage loop from Sant Quirze, a difficult 24.0 miles (38.6 km) path. This trail leads through forested areas and includes challenging climbs towards the Sant Medir Hermitage. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Torre Bonica – Camí del Terrer de Boscosa loop from Sant Quirze, a 12.2 miles (19.6 km) trail leading through wooded sections and offering viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Sant Quirze Del Vallès is defined by the forested hills of Serra de Galliners, urban parks, and historic paths like the Camí dels Monjos. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging excursions. The routes in Sant Quirze Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Quirze Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Sant Quirze Del Vallès?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Sant Quirze Del Vallès, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 500 times, contributing to their high average rating of 4.5 stars.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sant Quirze Del Vallès?
A: Sant Quirze Del Vallès offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. The region's climate is generally mild, making spring and autumn particularly pleasant with comfortable temperatures and lush landscapes. Even in winter, many trails remain accessible, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Sant Quirze Del Vallès?
A: Yes, there are a couple of easy mountain bike trails available. While many routes offer varied terrain, beginners can find suitable options to enjoy the natural beauty of the area. These trails typically feature less challenging ascents and descents, allowing for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. Sant Quirze Del Vallès offers several difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri – Sant Medir Hermitage loop from Sant Quirze&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 24-mile path with significant climbs through forested areas. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Torres farmhouse – Sant Llorenç Savall loop from Les Fonts&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 53 miles and includes substantial elevation gains.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: While many trails in Sant Quirze Del Vallès involve varied terrain, the Parc de les Morisques offers an extensive urban park with natural spaces and marked botanical routes that could be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous cycling experience. For dedicated mountain biking, look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' and consider the length and elevation gain to match your family's ability.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails around Sant Quirze Del Vallès?
A: Many natural areas in the Vallès region, including parts of the Serra de Galliners, are generally dog-friendly, especially on trails where dogs can be kept under control. However, it's always recommended to check specific local regulations or signage for each trail, particularly within protected areas or private lands, to ensure compliance.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails offer access to several natural attractions and viewpoints. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri&lt;/a&gt;, a monumental tree, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Torre Bonica – Camí del Terrer de Boscosa loop from Sant Quirze&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers scenic viewpoints. For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that lead towards La Mola (1050 meters), where on clear days you can even glimpse the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Sant Quirze Del Vallès are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938032033/serra-de-galliners-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Galliners Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 21.8-mile trail that provides varied terrain and panoramic views. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38145009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Mola – Can Torres farmhouse loop from Sant Quirze&lt;/a&gt;, a more difficult 31.9-mile route.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Sant Quirze Del Vallès?
A: Sant Quirze Del Vallès is well-integrated into the region, and parking is generally available in and around the town center, as well as at various access points to the Serra de Galliners. Specific parking areas may vary depending on your chosen starting point for a trail, so it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for recommended parking spots.

Q: Is it possible to access mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Sant Quirze Del Vallès is situated within the broader Vallès region, which has public transport connections. While direct access to every trailhead via public transport might be limited, the town itself is accessible by train (FGC) and bus, allowing you to reach a starting point and then cycle to the trails. Check local public transport schedules for the most convenient options.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs near the mountain bike trails for a break?
A: Sant Quirze Del Vallès and its surrounding villages offer various cafes and pubs where you can take a break and refuel. While some trails might lead through more remote natural areas, you'll find amenities in the urban centers and smaller towns along or near the routes. It's a good idea to plan your route to include stops in these areas if you wish to visit a cafe or pub.

Q: Do I need any special permits for mountain biking in Sant Quirze Del Vallès?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for mountain biking on established trails in the natural areas around Sant Quirze Del Vallès, such as the Serra de Galliners. However, always respect local regulations, especially when riding through private land or designated protected zones. Stay on marked paths to minimize environmental impact.
tips: The Serra de Galliners is a mountain range between the municipalities of Sant Quirze del Vallès and Terrassa in the region of Vallès Occidental, with a maximum elevation of 315.8 meters. [1] It is located in the middle of the Vallès depression, divides the Vallès plain in two and delimits the Besòs (A llevant) and Llobregat (west) basins. The materials of the mountain range are identical to those of the plain, although less eroded. It is an alternation of thin and thick sedimentary materials with little cementation of the Miocene. [2] It is a biological corridor between the Coastal Range and the Prelitoral. This area is one of the last forest areas on the Vallès plain. You can still find a rich fauna that due to the human activities that take place there (motorized access, hunting, sports activities) often goes unnoticed. The typical vegetation of the mountain range is that of the Mediterranean forest, such as the holm oak grove dominated by holm oak accompanied by buckthorn and strawberry and white pine or pine groves. On the northern slope of the mountains the holm oak grove is sometimes mixed with oak with the presence of hazel and hawthorn. [3] Some of the most outstanding elevations are the hill of Can Camps (296 meters) and the hill of Mojal (312 meters), both at the end of Sant Quirze del Vallès. [2]
This path is short but fun and has many alternatives that you can do. I highly recommend it.

Elevation of the land located in the Serra de Galliners, at 315 m altitude. It is considered the highest peak. Until a few years ago, there was a tower with a metal structure at the top used, during the summer, as a forest lookout point, to prevent and detect fires. You can still see, also on top of the hill, the zigzag trenches that were dug there during the last civil war.

   On the part that overlooks the Camí dels Monjos there is a tidy viewpoint, from where you can contemplate an extensive panorama that embraces a good part of our Vallès, with the most prominent peaks of the Prelitoral mountain range: Sant Llorenç del Munt, Puig de la Creu, Farell, the cliffs of Gallifa and Montseny. Montserrat can also be seen, partially.

   On days of good visibility from here you can see the Mediterranean Sea through the Montcada Pass and the mouth of the Besòs River.
This horse is a good place close to many paths. If you go there with MTB, watch out for the horses.
Fast fun trail. You can do it in both directions.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route, and is it suitable for all skill levels?
faqs.answer: The Ruta del evento OPEN NATURA SANT QUIRZE VALLES is rated as moderate. While it's designed to be enjoyable for a range of riders, it does require a minimum level of skill and good physical condition. Some sections, particularly the 'senderos y trialeras' (paths and technical trails), might be more challenging for less experienced riders. The event itself aims for 'maximum fun accessible to all levels' by often offering varied circuit lengths.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain, including a mix of 'senderos y trialeras' (paths and technical singletracks). A notable section is the 'Trialera de la Serra de Galliners,' described as a 'Fast Fun-Trail.' There are also 'arcillosa' (clayey) areas. The route is known for well-crafted paths that offer a pure mountain bike experience.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses beautiful landscapes and forests. A significant highlight is the ascent to the Turó de Mataric, which is part of the Serra de Galliners. From this highest point, you can enjoy panoramic views of the Vallès region, including prominent peaks like Sant Llorenç del Munt and Montseny. On clear days, you might even spot Montserrat and the Mediterranean Sea. You'll also pass the Pujol Blanc Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The starting and finishing point for the Open Natura events is commonly at the Masia de Can Feliu in Sant Quirze del Vallès. While specific parking details for this exact route aren't provided, it's generally advisable to look for local parking near this area. Public transport options to Sant Quirze del Vallès are also available, though may require a short ride to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta del evento OPEN NATURA SANT QUIRZE VALLES dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Open Natura events are often family-friendly, but specific rules regarding dogs on the mountain bike route are not detailed. Generally, when mountain biking with dogs, they should be well-behaved, under strict control, and on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users. Always check local regulations or event-specific rules if participating in an organized event.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. However, for mountain biking, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential wet, muddy conditions in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Serra de Galliners area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails in the Serra de Galliners area. However, if you are participating in the organized 'Open Natura Sant Quirze Vallès' event, there will be a registration fee for the event itself, which covers organization, aid stations, and often includes a charitable contribution. For casual rides, no fees are typically needed.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sender del Vallès Natural, Sender de Montserrat, and the Circular d'Ègara - Terrassa, among others. It also crosses the Camí dels Monjos and the GR 173 at various points.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Sant Quirze del Vallès, a town that offers various amenities. While there might not be facilities directly on the trail, you can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the town itself for pre- or post-ride refreshments. The starting point at Masia de Can Feliu might also have some facilities during organized events.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and plenty of water. It's also wise to carry basic repair tools, a first-aid kit, navigation tools (like the komoot app), and snacks. Consider bringing layers of clothing as conditions can change, especially when gaining elevation.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or unique features to look out for?
faqs.answer: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can observe historical zigzag trenches from the Civil War on the hilltops, particularly around the Serra de Galliners. The route also passes by Hípica Can Buimira, an equestrian center.
